diagnostics.message: shader declares 5 resource bindings but no `[RootSignature(...)]` attribute is present -- compiling with `-all-resources-bound` (D3D12) unlocks driver optimisations that skip per-resource null-binding checks on every IHV
cbuffer `RaytrCB` (96 bytes) is too large for root constants but well under the 64 KB cbuffer limit -- consider binding as a root CBV to dodge the descriptor-table indirection on every dispatch

sampler `LinearSS` is referenced from a single entry point and never varies across draws -- consider promoting it to a D3D12 static sampler in the root signature (saves a descriptor heap slot, pre-resident on every IHV)
struct used as a DXR ray payload is 152 bytes (> 32-byte register-only ceiling) -- payload spills to the on-chip ray stack on every `TraceRay`, costing latency on every leaf-traversal step

`TraceRay` in shadow / occlusion-style function omits `RAY_FLAG_ACCEPT_FIRST_HIT_AND_END_SEARCH` -- BVH traversal continues past the first hit even though only the occlusion bit is needed; the flag short-circuits and saves a full BVH walk per ray
`if` whose condition looks dynamically uniform lacks an explicit `[branch]` attribute -- without the hint the compiler may flatten the branch and execute both arms

(suggestion) `Sample()` result `albedo` is consumed within the next 1 statement(s) -- interleave compute between sample and use to hide the texture-cache miss latency (Nsight: "Warp Stalled by L1 Long Scoreboard")

`SampleLevel(s, uv, 0)` pins LOD to mip 0 -- on a mipped texture this defeats the texture cache's small-footprint fetch and disables trilinear filtering; replace with `Sample(s, uv)` in pixel-shader context
`if` whose condition looks dynamically uniform lacks an explicit `[branch]` attribute -- without the hint the compiler may flatten the branch and execute both arms
`dot(v, axis)` against an axis-aligned constant unit vector is just a swizzle (`v.y`) -- saves a multiply-add chain and avoids loading the constant vector
`pow(x, 32.0)` should be replaced with a pow-by-squaring chain -- `pow` lowers to a transcendental sequence on every current GPU, but a handful of multiplies is strictly cheaper
